question.text: Which of the following is a prohibited method of buyback under SEBI Regulations?

answer.text: Negotiated deals whether on or off the stock exchange
Open market through book building process
Open market purchases through the stock exchange

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI explicitly prohibits buyback through negotiated deals (whether on or off the stock exchange), spot transactions, and private arrangements. Only tender offers and open market methods (through book building or stock exchange) are permitted. **The Trap:** Open market purchases through the stock exchange are a permitted method (though restricted from April 1, 2025), so candidates might incorrectly think all open market methods are prohibited. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX specifically tests prohibited buyback methods; NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) tests similar restrictions for mutual fund share transactions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Negotiated deals are banned to ensure equal treatment of all shareholders — this is the fairness principle underlying buyback regulations.
